LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED TO MAY 22, 2014, ON THE COURT'S MOTION. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE EXISTS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING THE EXISTENCE OF AN EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP AND/OR WHETHER MR. TRAN WAS THE OSTENSIBLE AGENT OF DEFENDANT YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. THEREFORE, THERE IS AN ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER DEFENDANT IS VICARIOUSLY LIABLE ON THE NEGLIGENCE AND ASSAULT/BATTERY CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE NEGLIGENT, HIRING, TRAINING, RETENTION, SUPERVISION, AND INSTRUCTION CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT NEVER PROVIDED ANY TRAINING TO MR. TRAN REGARDING HOW TO HANDLE A FARE DISPUTE (PLAINTIFF'S FACT 181) AND DID NOTHING TO PREVENT MR. TRAN FROM CARRYING A TIRE IRON IN THE DRIVER'S COMPARTMENT (PLAINTIFF'S FACT 182). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: JOYCE FINLEY #8620.
ORDER DENYING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2014 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-18-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-13-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt Summ Adj
Off Calendar May-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Khaldoun Baghdadi, Esq., 415.981.7210, for plaintiff Eduardo Bustamante.
Gregory Huber, Esq., 415.352.6416, for defendant Yellow Cab Cooperative, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There exists a triable issue of material fact regarding the existence of an employment relationship and/or whether Mr. Tran was the ostensible agent of defendant Yellow Cab Cooperative, Inc. Therefore, there is an issue of fact whether defendant is vicariously liable on the negligence and assault/battery causes of action. The court denies summary adjudication on the negligent, hiring, training, retention, supervision, and instruction cause of action. Defendant never provided any training to Mr. Tran regarding how to handle a fare dispute (Plaintiff's Fact 181) and did nothing to prevent Mr. Tran from carrying a tire iron in the driver's compartment (Plaintiff's fact 182). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
